title: Protocol: China's clinical trial leapfrog and lessons for Africa's top five trial-producing countries
body: This cross-sectional registry study will compare China's interventional clinical trial output against Africa's five highest-producing countries using ClinicalTrials.gov. We will query the API v2 for interventional trials per country using filter.advanced with AREA[StudyType]INTERVENTIONAL and query.locn, with 500-millisecond rate-limiting. Primary countries are China, Egypt, South Africa, Uganda, Kenya, and Nigeria, with the United States, India, and Brazil as benchmarks. For each country we will compute per-capita density, Phase 1-4 distribution, condition-specific counts across HIV, cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease, stroke, and traditional medicine, and year-by-year growth from 2010 to 2025. Secondary analyses will compare Phase 1 share as a proxy for domestic drug development and identify 2015 as a candidate inflection point. Results will be cached and rendered as a dark-theme interactive dashboard. Limitations include single-registry restriction, inability to attribute growth causally to specific policies, and potential undercount of African-led trials on local platforms.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

references.citation: National Medical Products Administration. Drug Registration Regulation (2020 revision). NMPA Order No. 27.
African Union. Treaty for the Establishment of the African Medicines Agency. Addis Ababa, 2019. Entered into force 2023.
Hedt-Gauthier BL, et al. Building research capacity through a hospital-based research partnership. BMC Glob Public Health. 2025;3:12.

checks.name: methods before outcomes
checks.detail: API query method, country selection, and metric definitions precede secondary analyses.

checks.name: limitations stated
checks.detail: Final sentence addresses single-registry limitation, causal attribution, and African undercount.
